description: Enum-KEY value-native map (§59.4). A [City:int] map keyed by an ENUM. §59.4 declares enum keys supported (structural tag+payload identity); §14.10 bare-variant inference resolves a bare .Variant in a map-KEY position against the map's declared key enum. A .insert(.Dallas, 4500) key and the .getOr(.Dallas, 0) / .getOr(.Houston, 0) / .has(.Dallas) key reads all resolve their bare .Variant against City — NO spurious E-VARIANT-AMBIGUOUS. Codegen lowers a bare .Variant to its string tag, so insert and read hash the same key: after the insert, DAL=4500, HOU=0 (default — .Houston absent), HasDAL=true. Observed via impl-neutral derived scalars + DOM (the raw map cell is an impl-internal HAMT, not asserted).
rationale: §59.4 a map key may be any §45-comparable value incl. enums; §14.10 flows the map's declared key-type into a bare .Variant in key position. insert(.Dallas,4500) then getOr(.Dallas,0)==4500, getOr(.Houston,0)==0, has(.Dallas)==true; no E-VARIANT-AMBIGUOUS.
